message:
Harden sandbox pre_exec for async-signal-safety and expand protocol frame test coverage

Linux sandbox (linux.rs): Refactored the pre_exec closure to be
async-signal-safe per POSIX requirements. All allocations and complex
operations now happen BEFORE the fork: seccomp filters are pre-computed,
Landlock CString paths are pre-allocated, and userns/landlock availability
flags are captured as plain bools. Inside pre_exec, only raw syscalls
(unshare, setrlimit, prctl) are used — no logging, no String formatting,
no heap allocation. Added direct rlimit enforcement for RLIMIT_DATA,
RLIMIT_CPU, RLIMIT_NOFILE, and RLIMIT_CORE. Landlock filesystem
restrictions now apply read-only and read-write paths using the
pre-allocated CStrings.

macOS sandbox (macos.rs): Similar pre_exec hardening. Sandbox profile
string is now pre-computed before fork. The sandbox_init call in
pre_exec uses only the pre-allocated CString. Added rlimit enforcement
matching the Linux implementation.

Egress (egress.rs): Expanded SSRF prevention test coverage with
additional edge cases for IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses and hostname
canonicalization.

Protocol — FCPC frames (fcpc.rs): Added 246 lines of tests covering
magic constant verification, tag length validation, default max payload
size, unknown flag bit truncation, header decode with trailing bytes,
empty slice rejection, round-trip encode/decode for various payload
sizes, sequence number preservation, and flag combinations.

Protocol — FCPS frames (fcps.rs): Added 266 lines of tests covering
FCPS magic constant, signature size, max payload defaults, header
encode/decode, empty payload handling, flag preservation, version
mismatch detection, and cryptographic sign/verify round-trips.

Protocol — Symbol envelope (symbol_envelope.rs): Added 177 lines of
tests covering envelope encode/decode, zone ID hash preservation,
ESI and SBN field handling, padding flag behavior, and AEAD
encrypt/decrypt round-trips with the envelope metadata as AAD.

Test vectors: Updated key_schedule.json and MAC suite hex files to
match the corrected HKDF derivation from the prior commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
